reference: This circular introduces relief measures for Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) affected by the Easter Sunday attacks, the COVID-19 pandemic and adverse macroeconomic conditions, to be provided by licensed commercial and specialised banks in agreement with the Sri Lanka Banks' Association. Eligible SME borrowers are those whose credit facilities were classified as Stage 3 on or after 01.04.2019 and who commenced business-revival discussions with the banks' Business Revival Units by 31.03.2025. Banks may reschedule impaired loans based on the borrower's repayment capacity and an acceptable revival plan, with reschedulement agreements to be entered into by 15.06.2025. Repayment of rescheduled loans must commence not later than 31.12.2025 for outstanding capital below Rs. 25 Mn, 30.09.2025 for Rs. 25-50 Mn, and 30.06.2025 for amounts above Rs. 50 Mn. Banks may also waive unpaid interest (excluding capitalised interest) accrued between 01.04.2019 and 15.12.2024. Restructured facilities must be reported to the Credit Information Bureau (CRIB), banks may not decline eligible applications solely on an adverse CRIB record, and rejected borrowers may appeal to the Director, Financial Consumer Relations Department of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka.

This Human Resource circular sets out the Bank of Ceylon's revised policy on remote work, effective 14 July 2025, and applies to all eligible employees. Remote work may be initiated by the Management or requested by the employee, subject to the Management's approval. A designated employee must enter into a Remote Work Agreement with the Bank before commencing remote work, specifying the terms, any special benefits, and supervisory responsibilities. Employees must satisfy the prerequisites in the Remote Work Approval Checklist and work from an Approved Remote Work Location that meets defined standards for workspace safety, electrical and fire safety, and illumination. Remote workers must clock in and out remotely to record attendance, remain available during agreed working hours, maintain the confidentiality of Bank information, and use supplied equipment solely for remote work; failure to record attendance is treated as unauthorised absence except during approved leave. The Bank accepts no liability for damage to employee-owned equipment, and work-related injuries must be reported immediately to the supervisor and Head of Department. The relevant forms are provided as Annexure II (Application Form), Annexure III (Approval Checklist) and Annexure IV (Remote Work Agreement).
